zh.lifeSummary: 當Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron於1205年出生在西班牙坎塔布里亞埃斯卡蘭特時，他的父親Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés27歲，母親Teresa de Girón20歲。他和Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa至少有1個兒子。他去世於1250年在西班牙享年45歲。

nameMeanings.name: Diego
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Origin uncertain; perhaps from Santiago or from a native Iberian name found in Latin as Didacus.

nameMeanings.name: Ruiz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Francisco, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Pedro, Ramon, Jorge.
Spanish and Portuguese: patronymic from the personal name Ruy, a short form of Rodrigo .

variants.name: Ruisi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Santo, Angelo, Matteo, Carmela, Lorenzo, Pasquale, Vittorio.
Italian (Sicily): probably an Italianized form of Spanish   Ruiz  .

variants.name: Ruz
meanings: Variant spelling of   Ros  , with the final consonant altered to represent the voiced sound of the names from which it derives.

variants.name: Rodrigo
meanings: Visigothic: composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame’ + rīc ‘power’. It was the name of the last king of the Visigoths, defeated by the Moors in 711 .

variants.name: Ruis
meanings: form of   Louis  , also used in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Ceballos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Juan, Carlos, Manuel, Francisco, Mario, Sergio, Javier, Jesus, Miguel, Ramon.
Spanish: habitational name from a place called Ceballos in Cantabria. Compare   Cevallos  ,   Zeballos  , and   Zevallos  .

variants.name: Ceballo
meanings: Spanish (mainly southern): probably an altered form (singular) of   Ceballos  .

variants.name: Cevallos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Pedro, Fernando, Jose, Luis, Eduardo, Francisco, Jaime, Ana, Angel.
Spanish: variant of   Ceballos  .

nameMeanings.name: Gonzalez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Jorge, Pedro, Francisco, Miguel, Ramon, Raul, Mario.
Spanish (González): patronymic from the personal name Gonzalo . Compare De Gonzalez , Gonsales , Gunsalus , and Portuguese Goncalves .

variants.name: Gonzelez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonsalez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Ruben, Ignacio, Manuel, Alfredo, Elena, Felipe, Guadalupe, Gustavo, Jesus, Jorge.
Hispanic (Gonsález): altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gongalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzolas
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonsales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Ruben, Arcadio, Artemio, Erasmo, Ernesto, Eusebio, Juan, Lupe, Manuel, Miguel.
Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzalo
meanings: Visigothic: from a personal name formed with gund ‘battle, strife’.

variants.name: De Gonzalez
meanings: Spanish (De González): patronymic from González (see Gonzalez ), with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Gozalez
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish González (see Gonzalez ).

variants.name: Gonzales
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Ramon, Ruben, Pedro, Raul, Guadalupe, Lupe.
Spanish: altered form of González (see Gonzalez ).

nameMeanings.name: Giron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Luis, Julio, Francisco, Jesus, Manuel, Ana, Jorge, Miguel, Mario.
French: variant of Géron (see Geron ).
French: from a pet form of the personal name Gire, a variant of Gilles .

variants.name: Jiron
meanings: ‘Second son’.

variants.name: Geron
meanings: Apparently from Greek gerōn ‘old man’; the name of a saint martyred in Cologne at the beginning of the 4th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron was born in 1205, in Escalante, Cantabria, Spain, his father, Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés, was 27 and his mother, Teresa de Girón, was 20. He had at least 1 son with Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. He died in 1250, in Spain, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron est né en 1205, à Escalante, Cantabria, Espagne, son père, Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Teresa de Girón, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. Il est décédé en 1250, à Espagne, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron 1205 in Escalante, Cantabria, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés 27 und seine Mutter Teresa de Girón 20. Er hatte mit Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1250 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron è nato il nel 1205 nel Escalante, Cantabria, Spagna, il suo padre, Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Teresa de Girón, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. Morì nel 1250, nel Spagna, all'età di 45 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Gironが1205年にスペインカンタブリアエスカラントで生まれた時，父Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclésは27歳，母Teresa de Girónは20歳であった。彼はMaria Nuñez de Finestrosaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1250年にスペインで45歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron(이)가 1205년 스페인 캔타브리아 스알랜티에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Teresa de Girón(은)는 20세였다. 그는 Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1250년 스페인에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron nasceu em 1205, em Escalante, Cantabria, Espanha, seu pai, Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Teresa de Girón, tinha 20 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. Ele faleceu em 1250, em Espanha, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron родился в 1205 г. в местности эскалант, кантабря, Испания, его отцу по имени Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Teresa de Girón, 20. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. Он умер в 1250 г. в местности Испания в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Diego Ruiz de Ceballos y Gonzalez Giron nació en 1205, en Escalante, Cantabria, España, su papá, Ruy o Rodrigo González de Cevallos e Hita Comendador O. Santiago Uclés, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Teresa de Girón, tenía 20 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Maria Nuñez de Finestrosa. Murió en 1250, en España, a la edad de 45 años.
